An interesting musical and liter


by the pupils of the Northwestern


Business college. Part of the pro-


gram was a debate on the limita-


ton of suffrage in the south, in
which there developed discussion
of the race question.


The late II. D. Skinner, a we'll
known old time resident, who died


Monday at his home, 1904 Nora
avenue. was buried this afternoon
at Paramount cemetery, with fun
eral services by the Rev. H. l..
Rasmus.


A congress Of parents, attended
by many Spokane people, was held
yesterday at the Cheney Normal,


under the direction of the prin-
cipal, Professor H. c. Sampson.


Charles Bennett, an Oregon man.,
and Miss Nellie O'Rourke, nurse
and graduate of the Sacred Heart
training school, will be married to-
morrow morning at at Our Lady
of Lourdes church. They will re-
side at La Grande, Ore.
Contracts for the concrete work
and other divisions of the construc-
ton of the new Shubert theater
will be awarded Saturday. The


building and grounds will represent
total investment of 100000.
Father Adams Of the faculty of
Gonzaga college will deliver a lee
ture before an open meeting of the
Knights of Columbus in Pacific
Halls Monday night on the subject,
1.-THE Theory of Evolution"


A union Thanksgiving service Of
the Congregational churches of the
city will be held tonight at 7:45
at the Westminster Congregational
church. The Rev. II. f. Mills will
